step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("greatest movement in joint types")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed evidence gathered, we see clear definitions and descriptions confirming that diarthroses represent freely movable joints allowing extensive movement compared to the restricted or absent movement found in amphiarthroses and synarthroses respectively. The text provides examples showing full-range movements permitted even in complex joints like the shoulder (glenohumeral) which exemplify the greatest potential for motion characteristic of true diarthrosis. Furthermore, references to uses of goniometry highlight variability in measuring joint angles precisely depending on joint type - again emphasizing differences favoring higher mobility observed uniquely in diarthroses vs others. Therefore I remain convinced our initial conclusion remains valid.
